title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 4.
content:
There was an Old Man on a hill,
    Who seldom, if ever, stood still;
    He ran up and down,
    In his Grandmother's gown,
    Which adorned that Old Man on a hill.
meaning:
"Book Of Nonsense Limerick 4." is a quintessential example of Edward Lear's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
